hypothesis: A fixed p/u zero phase might forbid some finite future branch drivers or finite dual-depth zero targets.
interpretation: Both fixed phases realize every finite future driver. Positive level transitivity supplies a zero-target witness, and a finite-order p or u identity prefix sets either requested phase without changing the depth-L action.
limitations: the phase-padded witness depends on the requested depth
does not construct one ordinary finite word surviving an infinite driver
does not prove the alternating inverse lift has infinite support
does not exclude eventual center period two
does not solve Rule 30 center nonperiodicity
proof_scope: Positive level transitivity and finite-order phase padding give the all-depth finite-phase universality theorem in proofs/informal/problem1_period_two_phase_universality.md. The breadth-first campaigns are bounded constructive verification.
question: problem1

exact_conclusions.finite_phase_universality: both fixed phases realize every finite future driver prefix
exact_conclusions.no_finite_language_obstruction: no forbidden finite branch word, finite dual-depth mismatch, or finite phase cylinder can exclude a final zero tail
exact_conclusions.remaining_target: an infinite actual-orbit argument must prevent one compatible sequence of finite phase witnesses from converging to an ordinary finite state

phase_padded_driver_universality.exact_conclusion: for every finite future driver and each phase p/u, a finite accumulated word of that phase realizes the complete terminal-zero prefix

positive_level_transitivity.exact_reason: the existing self-replication theorem gives level transitivity; on each finite level generator inverses are positive powers, so the positive monoid has the same orbit

result_summary.scope_warning: the witness depends on the requested depth; finite-depth universality does not construct one ordinary word surviving an infinite driver
